_comment: Encoded-state value -> meaning map (docs/02 §8). Tekken 8's entity struct does not carry the
per-flag booleans PlayerFrame wants (block_stun, hit_stun, airborne, ...). It carries a handful
of ENCODED STATE WORDS whose integer values each denote a whole situation. This file is the
value -> meaning map: outer key = the player field, inner key = the raw integer as a string,
value = the semantic flags that value implies.
It is a SEPARATE file from assets/offsets/<version>.json on purpose. `update-offsets` rewrites
the ADDRESSES every build; these SEMANTICS are calibrated once by observation and carried
forward. The builder copies this map into each table it writes.
It ships UNCALIBRATED (`calibrated: false`, every `flags` map empty). An unmapped raw value
contributes no flags, so with this file as shipped the reader RUNS but every action_state
decodes to `neutral` and every stun/throw/juggle flag is false — structurally valid, semantically
empty. `update-offsets` and `doctor` say so loudly. The raw integers ride out on
PlayerFrame.raw_state (docs/03 §1) so a captured frame tells you exactly what is unmapped.
WHY OBSERVATION AND NOT A LOOKUP: the offsets above are facts derivable from a layout data file,
but the round-start Jin-vs-Kazuya oracle cannot prove what stun_type==3 MEANS, because nobody is
in stun at round start. See docs/02 §8 for the protocol that fills this in (block a jab, eat a
jab, get staggered, get thrown, ... and record the raw values), and docs/02 §5 for why the
source of these values is a licensing question, not just an engineering one.
Known flags (offsets.STATE_FLAGS): neutral, attack, recovery, block_stun, hit_stun, stagger,
throw_active, throw_tech, thrown, airborne, juggle, knockdown, wakeup, sidestep, crouch.
An unknown flag name is rejected at load time, not silently dropped at 60fps.
notes: Uncalibrated skeleton. Fill via the docs/02 §8 observation protocol: `python -m tekken_coach.reader.commands probe-state` while performing each state, then map the raw values here.
